message:
feat(fcp-e2e): improve host_e2e replay commands, external transcript recording, and evidence bundles

Replace bogus hardcoded replay test target with optional `replay_test_filter`
field on `SessionE2eConfig`. When a concrete cargo test filter is provided,
the replay command now points at the real owning test surface instead of a
non-existent canned target. When absent, the command includes an explanatory
comment directing the operator to use the host-facing simulate command or
supply a filter manually.

Rework `record_external_transcript` to run the full setup → lifecycle →
execute → verify → teardown phase pipeline instead of a flat verification
stub. The execute phase duration now reflects the actual externally-reported
transcript duration rather than the wall-clock time of the recording call,
which makes aggregate duration_ms truthful for externally executed runs.

Add a new checkpoint evidence step to `build_evidence_bundle` that captures
structured phase logs (as JSONL), transcript outcome assertion, and metric
evidence items (phase_log_count, transcript_step_count,
transcript_duration_ms) plus a HealthSnapshot for the connector component.
This makes evidence bundles self-contained for triage without requiring
access to the original test logs.

Apply rustfmt formatting fixes (line wrapping on multi-argument function
signatures, format! macro arguments, and tuple returns).

New tests:
- `replay_command_uses_explicit_test_filter_when_available`: verifies the
  replay_test_filter is wired through to the replay command output
- `external_transcript_records_all_phases_and_checkpoint_evidence`: verifies
  all 5 phases are logged, execute_ms matches the transcript duration,
  total duration_ms is the sum of all phases, and the checkpoint step
  contains Log, Metric, and HealthSnapshot evidence items
- Updated `config_serialization_roundtrip` to cover replay_test_filter
  serde roundtrip
- Updated `replay_command_includes_fixture_addr` assertions to validate
  absence of bogus test target

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl to reflect notes on issue 49z0b.7.2
(runtime/host/streaming/webhook lifecycle acceptance sweep) documenting the
host_e2e.rs patches and remaining blocker (recursive .beads recovery
snapshots exhausting disk during rch artifact retrieval).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","title":"Platform matrix, skip policy, and host_e2e adoption guide for stateful and local fixtures","description":"Background:
- Some of these fixtures are platform-specific or only safe in certain environments, which means the run matrix has to be explicit before later sweeps begin.

Deliverables:
- Publish the platform matrix, skip rules, cleanup guarantees, and host_e2e adoption guidance for DB/storage/queue/browser/local fixtures.
- Make it obvious which suites can run in generic CI, which require dedicated hosts, and how failures should be triaged.

Acceptance criteria:
- Platform constraints are documented up front instead of buried inside flaky tests or tribal knowledge.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.021266Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:58:47Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:58:47Z","close_reason":"Published the platform matrix, skip policy, cleanup guarantees, and host_e2e adoption guidance for DB/storage/queue/browser/local fixtures in docs/testing/coverage-inventory.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","e2e","fixtures","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:24.031303Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.021266Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:23.554232Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.343312Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","title":"Core platform crates: expand non-mock coverage and host-backed verification","description":"Background:
- The connectors are only as trustworthy as the platform crates underneath them.
- Some crates already have `no_mock_integration.rs`, golden vectors, host integration suites, and log-schema checks, but the coverage is uneven.

Scope:
- `fcp-core`, `fcp-protocol`, `fcp-crypto`, `fcp-cbor`, `fcp-manifest`, `fcp-sdk`, `fcp-host`, `fcp-store`, `fcp-mesh`, `fcp-webhook`, `fcp-oauth`, `fcp-telemetry`, `fcp-ratelimit`, and adjacent runtime-support crates that materially affect connector behavior.

Deliverables:
- Expand no-mock integration and host-backed coverage for lifecycle, policy, receipts, cancellation, restart/recovery, rollout, telemetry, redaction, and schema/logging guarantees.
- Close gaps where current coverage stops at golden vectors or mock-backed helpers.
- Make core-crate failures easy to localize via structured artifacts rather than giant opaque test logs.

Acceptance criteria:
- The platform layer has an explicit acceptance story independent of any single connector.
- Core regressions are caught before connector-family sweeps start blaming downstream crates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:35.758018Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-24T06:03:41Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:35.758018Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:40.223334Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:40.455275Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:40.696564Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","title":"Protocol, manifest, crypto, and core semantic crate truth sweep","description":"Background:
- The protocol, manifest, and crypto crates define the invariants every connector inherits.
- Their verification cannot stop at golden vectors that are hard to map back to operator-facing failures.

Deliverables:
- Sweep `fcp-core`, `fcp-protocol`, `fcp-crypto`, `fcp-cbor`, and `fcp-manifest` for missing pure-unit floors, vector coverage, schema conformance, and non-mock validation where applicable.
- Make failure output specific enough that downstream connector sweeps can trust the core layer.

Acceptance criteria:
- Core semantic crates have a truthful and debuggable verification story independent of any one connector.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.424447Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","updated_at":"2026-03-23T19:35:30.088774Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T19:35:30.088438Z","close_reason":"Sweep complete: ~11,991 tests across 5 core crates. No wiremock leakage. 3 clean no_mock suites. Fixed duplicate test. Golden vectors cover 13+ domains.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.16.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:24.313261Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:24.612769Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.424447Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1892,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"MagentaStream (2026-03-23): Core crate truth sweep complete. 5 crates: fcp-core (4300+ tests, golden vectors for 13 domains, MockProvisioner local-mock acceptable), fcp-protocol (3931+ tests, FCPC/FCPS/session vectors, clean no_mock_integration), fcp-crypto (513+ tests, Ed25519/X25519/HPKE/Shamir, clean no_mock_integration), fcp-cbor (540+ tests, canonical CBOR vectors), fcp-manifest (573+ tests, manifest vectors, clean no_mock_integration). Total: ~11,991 tests across lib+tests. Fixed pre-existing duplicate test name in fcp-core/capability.rs (instance_id_display). No wiremock leakage in any of these 5 crates. 3/3 no_mock_integration.rs files verified clean. Verification story is truthful and debuggable.","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:34:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","title":"Store, mesh, tailscale, telemetry, and durability acceptance sweep","description":"Background:
- Store, mesh, tailscale, telemetry, audit, and durability crates influence delivery guarantees, repair behavior, and observability across every acceptance suite.

Deliverables:
- Sweep `fcp-store`, `fcp-mesh`, `fcp-tailscale`, `fcp-telemetry`, `fcp-audit`, `fcp-raptorq`, and adjacent durability crates for truthful stateful acceptance coverage and clearer artifacts.
- Cover repair, restart, propagation, logging, and recovery cases that connector tests currently assume are working.

Acceptance criteria:
- Mesh/durability regressions can be isolated at the crate layer instead of surfacing only as connector-family noise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.866029Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","updated_at":"2026-03-24T02:04:00.580778Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T02:04:00.580455Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.16.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:26.143220Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:26.445095Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.509567Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:25.851256Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:13.866029Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1901,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-03-23: Added 12 new repair convergence acceptance tests to fcp-store (evaluate_zone, plan_zone, queue convergence, concurrent permits, rate limiting, deduplication, diversity deficit). Added 9 new telemetry export acceptance tests (JSON contract, mixed health scenarios, prometheus format). Fixed fcp-testkit streaming_fixture.rs extra brace. Total: 87/87 matrix tests, 756/756 fcp-store repair tests, 53/53 telemetry export tests — all pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-24T02:03:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.4","title":"Core-platform evidence packs, rerun recipes, and regression triage docs","description":"Background:
- Even if the crate sweeps land, future agents need one consolidated evidence and rerun story for the platform layer.

Deliverables:
- Collect the core-platform artifact bundle shape, rerun commands, known-flake notes, and regression triage guidance into one maintainable lane.
- Make the platform layer easy to verify before and after downstream connector work.

Acceptance criteria:
- The platform crates have a single high-signal evidence index instead of scattered ad hoc references.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.096347Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","updated_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.871923Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","docs","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.096347Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.601182Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.740766Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:30:14.871330Z","created_by":"ChartreuseDesert","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","title":"Messaging and community connectors: replace mock-only confidence with real acceptance paths","description":"Background:
- Messaging/community connectors are the place where mock-only confidence breaks down fastest because session behavior matters as much as payload schemas.
- The current family includes both connectors with existing compliance E2E and connectors still missing it, and many still rely heavily on mock-backed integration coverage.

Scope:
- Slack, Discord, Telegram, Signal, WhatsApp, Matrix, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Synology Chat, IRC, QQ, Nostr, WeCom, DingTalk, Teams, Line, Feishu, BlueBubbles, iMessage, and other session-oriented community or bridge-backed messaging surfaces.

Deliverables:
- For each connector, add a required non-mock acceptance path that exercises connection lifecycle, auth/bridge prerequisites, join/send/receive semantics, retries, reconnects, attachments/media, and logging/redaction behavior.
- Convert existing mock-backed suites into supplemental contract tests where they remain useful, but ensure they are no longer the only evidence.
- Fill known family gaps where connectors currently lack compliance E2E coverage entirely.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every connector in the family is mapped to a truthful local fixture and/or nightly live path.
- Messaging regressions are diagnosable from durable artifacts instead of ad hoc stdout.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:36.110884Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-24T06:03:22Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","messaging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:36.110884Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:40.920064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:41.156174Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:41.396524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T09:40:18.339982Z","created_by":"WildFern","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:41.634634Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:41.872122Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
